title: China-to-Mozambique Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 61000 Series and IEC 62920 Evidence for Mozambique PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Mozambique PV inverter EMC evidence should be prepared on an IEC-referenced basis for INNOQ conformity/import review, EDM grid-connected project review, and lender or owner-engineer specifications. IEC 62920 is the dedicated EMC standard for PV power conversion equipment, while IEC 61000-series standards are commonly used for emissions, immunity, harmonics, flicker, and installation-level compatibility requirements. Where a solar project is financed by a development bank or donor, the lender technical schedule may require IEC EMC reports even when local import documentation is less explicit.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility standards for emissions, immunity, harmonics, flicker, and power-quality interfaces
INNOQ conformity/import review and project-specific EDM, lender, or owner-engineer specifications
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC documentation is commonly tied to GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, and related domestic laboratory reports. These documents can help explain the product design, but the limits, test configuration, reporting format, and acceptance route may differ from IEC 62920 or the IEC 61000-series package requested by INNOQ, EDM, project owners, or lenders in Mozambique.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC-related technical requirements
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Chinese EMC reports should be mapped line by line against IEC 62920 and the applicable IEC 61000-series tests before being used in Mozambique. Exporters should obtain or refresh IEC EMC type-test reports for the exact inverter model, power rating, firmware, and configuration, and should confirm whether testing at Mozambique-relevant 220/380 V, 50 Hz conditions is needed for the EDM or lender submission.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where INNOQ conformity/import review, EDM project review, or project-owner/lender specifications call for IEC EMC evidence; otherwise confirm case-by-case with the importer and competent authority
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese domestic EMC testing under GB/T or NB/T standards is not automatic IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-series acceptance for Mozambique

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For Mozambique PV inverter projects, prepare IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000-series EMC evidence instead of relying on China-only GB/T 37408-2021 or NB/T 32004 EMC summaries. Confirm with INNOQ, EDM, the importer, and any lender engineer whether the model requires fresh EMC testing under 220/380 V, 50 Hz project conditions.

rows.requirement: ARENE Licensing and EDM Grid Connection — IEC 62116, IEC 61727, and 220/380 V 50 Hz Settings
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied PV inverter deployment in Mozambique should be treated as a regulated electricity activity requiring confirmation of ARENE licensing obligations and EDM connection requirements. Mozambique's public grid is commonly specified as 220/380 V, 50 Hz. Inverter protection settings, anti-islanding behavior, reconnection delay, voltage/frequency trip points, and utility-interface performance must be reviewed against EDM's project connection conditions and the applicable IEC evidence package, especially IEC 62116 and IEC 61727. Large hydro from Cahora Bassa coexists with low electrification and growing donor, IPP, off-grid, mini-grid, mining, and Cabo Delgado gas-sector demand, so project-specific connection and lender requirements can be decisive.
target_standards: ARENE electricity-sector licensing and regulatory requirements for Mozambique
EDM project-specific grid-connection requirements for Mozambique's 220/380 V, 50 Hz network
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power stations, NB/T 32004 for grid-connected PV inverters, GB/T 37408-2021 technical evidence, and domestic anti-islanding or protection-setting reports. These are calibrated to Chinese grid operator requirements and China domestic project approvals. They do not create ARENE licensing clearance or EDM connection acceptance in Mozambique.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese grid certificates and factory firmware settings do not transfer to Mozambique. Exporters should confirm whether the project requires ARENE licensing or registration, obtain EDM project connection requirements, validate firmware against Mozambique's 220/380 V, 50 Hz grid, and provide IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence for the specific inverter model. Where the project is donor- or lender-financed, the lender's technical annex may impose IEC evidence beyond the minimum local connection package.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where ARENE licensing, EDM connection approval, or project-owner/lender grid-connection specifications apply; exact obligations depend on project size, connection type, and regulatory classification
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection approvals satisfy Chinese domestic requirements only and are not transferable to ARENE or EDM without Mozambique project acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on China GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, or factory default grid settings as Mozambique grid-connection evidence. Confirm ARENE licensing, obtain EDM connection conditions, and validate IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 evidence and firmware settings for the 220/380 V, 50 Hz network before project submission.

rows.requirement: EDM Project Connection and Rural / Mini-Grid Programmes — Documentation Gate for Financed Solar
rows.target_requirement: Mozambique solar projects are not limited to ordinary product import. Utility-scale plants such as Mocuba and Metoro, rural electrification programmes, off-grid and mini-grid schemes, and C&I systems for mining or LNG-linked demand often require EDM technical review, owner engineer approval, and donor or development-finance documentation. These packages commonly ask for IEC-referenced inverter datasheets, protection-setting tables, commissioning procedures, grid-code declarations, and test certificates rather than China-only certification summaries.
target_standards: EDM project connection and commissioning requirements
IEC 62116 — anti-islanding evidence for utility-interconnected PV inverters
IEC 61727 — utility-interface evidence for photovoltaic systems
Lender, donor, and owner-engineer specifications for Mozambique PV projects
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic distributed or utility PV approvals are based on Chinese grid-company rules and domestic standards such as GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, and GB/T 37408-2021. Those approvals may show product maturity but do not satisfy EDM project review, Mozambique rural electrification programme requirements, or lender technical annexes unless explicitly accepted.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Mozambique project documentation often needs model-specific IEC certificates, commissioning records, firmware settings, and site-specific protection coordination in addition to import paperwork. Exporters should align with the EPC, EDM, the owner engineer, and the lender before shipment so the inverter certificate package matches the actual project scale and connection arrangement.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for EDM-connected, donor-financed, IPP, rural electrification, mini-grid, C&I, mining, and LNG-linked projects where the owner engineer or lender requires IEC evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese project approvals and product certificates are background evidence only unless the Mozambique project owner, EDM, and lender accept them

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For Mozambique solar projects, EDM connection and lender documentation can be as important as import conformity. Build the inverter dossier around project-specific IEC certificates, protection settings, and commissioning evidence rather than China-only approvals, and confirm acceptance with EDM, the EPC, and the owner engineer before shipment.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — INNOQ NM/IEC Conformity and Project Specifications
rows.target_requirement: Mozambique's standards body is INNOQ, the Instituto Nacional de Normalização e Qualidade. INNOQ administers Mozambican standards (NM) and can adopt or reference IEC standards for electrical products and conformity/import certification. For PV inverters, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the normal safety benchmark requested in donor-financed, IPP, mini-grid, C&I, mining, and gas-sector solar procurement specifications. Where a product is imported or supplied into a regulated project, exporters should treat INNOQ conformity/import certification, project-owner specifications, and lender technical requirements as separate gates from the factory's China domestic certification.
target_standards: NM standards administered by INNOQ, including adopted or referenced IEC standards where applicable
IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
Project-owner and lender technical specifications for Mozambique solar PV, mini-grid, mining, and gas-sector projects
rows.china_equivalent: China PV inverter safety evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021 and NB/T 32004 test or certification documents issued through Chinese domestic laboratories and certification bodies. These documents may be useful technical background, but they are not an INNOQ conformity decision, not an IEC 62109-1/-2 certificate, and not automatic acceptance for Mozambique project-owner or lender specifications.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T or NB/T safety documents should not be submitted as a substitute for Mozambique-facing IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence without prior acceptance from INNOQ, the importer, the project owner, and any lender engineer. Exporters should prepare model-specific IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test reports or certificates, map them to the exact inverter model and firmware version, and confirm whether INNOQ conformity/import certification requires additional local documentation, Portuguese labeling, or inspection steps.

mandatory_status.target: Potentially mandatory through INNOQ conformity/import certification, project procurement specifications, and lender technical requirements; confirm the current NM/IEC adoption and product scope before shipment
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese GB/T 37408-2021 and NB/T 32004 evidence may support domestic China certification or procurement, but does not create automatic Mozambique market access

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat IEC 62109-1/-2 safety evidence as the practical baseline for Mozambique PV inverter supply, especially for INNOQ conformity/import review and financed projects. Chinese GB/T 37408-2021 or NB/T 32004 evidence is not automatically accepted. Confirm model-specific IEC evidence, Portuguese documentation needs, and INNOQ import/conformity steps before shipment.

source.publisher: INNOQ (Instituto Nacional de Normalização e Qualidade)
